messages.content: Useful advise

Yes but I firmly believe in one thing :
1. Indications from the body
2. Indications from events in the outer world.

If these two are not co-operating, it is best to pause, even if for months at a stretch. Without any guilt feeling whatsoever.

Stop, pause, reflect, but not think too deeply either.

When everything is okay once again, one slides effortlessly into one's usual routine. The word "effortless" is very important.

At such uneven times, I usually go back to writing software, my timepass hobby ð.

Then I just wait.

Difficult to start
Difficult to continue
And most difficult is to remain there or grow from there

Tamas is v strong and keeps pulling u back unto itself
Plus the responsibilities and pressure if earning money
And keep. Pulling u back
Plus people around u are still living that world u were once part of
The route is extemely slippery
When u reach a certain state of dhyan gyan bhakti how to stay therr or keep growing from there
Reacted ð¢ to your message
We r facing similar issues it seems

Firstly, is dhyan possible in kalyug ? This is precisely my problem now ð¤£ð¤£ð¤£. As soon as I can come back to a daily meditation routine, I will be fine. But I don't force it, so everyday a right time and moment is slipping by, somehow.

To answer the other part, this is the sequence :

A daily routine of meditation ( karma) leads to gyan which then leads to bhakti. True bhakti is almost a result. However, one can start the meditation with a kind of "doubtful bhakti", purely for its utilitarian usage.

This is only my personal opinion.
Udho had this problem for which Krishna sent him to. Gopis.

Where does dhyan stand btwn gyan and bhakti.
Is dhyan possible in kalyug
Or is ir even possible for him/her
How can a knowledge oriented person move towards bhakti

You have given me a topic for a post ð.

No no I am not unhappy about life events, it is something else, I am not able to pinpoint it. Probably this cold weather, body reaction to it. So my daily routine is disturbed and when that happens I go into a shell.
But then maturity lies in not making our happiness depend on life events or their outcomes

Yesssss, exactly. This has been my problem for long. I do get long stretches of relaxation nowadays. Aarey my problem is our son is at Bangalore, and every few months we have to shuttle between Delhi and Bangalore.
People like us seek solitude but are too duty conscious to not do the wordly chores

All fine. Aarey I am a bit bored of writing posts. Maybe I will write a few.

Thoda sa kuchh din se busy bhi huun, I don't exactly know why. Many small small "family duties" pending I guess.
No more posts on fb

There are many different ways of expressing this but essentially the same thing - anything eaten without being first eaten by the god is poison.

Action (karma) and knoweldge (gyan) when garnished by meditation (dhyan) and/or bhakti become forsaken (nishkaam). That's why sanatan says that every action of ours we shud attribute to God. (bhagwan ke nimitt kar dena)
My thought processes resonate so much with yours. Every time I feel as if a very evolved version of myself is writing it.

Remember here she is taking the teacher-student as the two people participating. But this mantra has a deeper meaning, where the subconscious is the teacher and the surface consciousness is the student.

She seems very good though ! Impressed how much learning is still hidden in certain schools in India !

This is the best pronunciation ( correct intonation, rise and fall of voice ) that I found on the internet. Listen only to the actual recitation part in the beginning. Her explanation is "optional".

https://youtu.be/NJlY4k4q3-4?feature=shared
Ok
But all that later.

It is somewhat important to pronounce it in the Sanskrit way, not in the Hindi way. See the breakups in the last attachment sent. For example, it is not à¤¨à¤¾à¤µ à¤à¤µà¤¤à¥ but à¤¨à¥ à¤à¤µà¤¤à¥  à¥¤ So, even this single mantra requires some study of the Sanskrit "break ups" to exactly get to the meaning.
As you wish. Maybe 3 times a day to start with. Only when mind is peaceful and comfortable. The idea is to "establish", à¤¸à¥à¤¥à¤¿à¤¤, à¤¸à¥à¤¥à¤¾, the mantra in your mind.
For what duration (silly Q I know)

Pronunciation wud be available on youtube
But pronunciation has to be as accurate as possible.

Yes. Best to chant it in a low voice, in a secluded place, with only your ears hearing your own voice uttering. Only a slight emphasis on the meaning, because the real test of any mantra is that its meaning becomes clearer with progress.
And shud I chant it or how to go about it
So here is your mantra. It is meant for joining the two.
What shud I do to. Connect with my sub conscious better
Because actually you know, perhaps far better than me. But you think you do not know because your surface self is not "joined" to your subconscious self.

"Wisdom is not communicable. The wisdom which a wise man tries to communicate always sounds foolish." Herman Hesse
Ok. Not about good or right it's just unnecessary suffering they are bringing upon themselves by sheer lack of understanding

How do you know your way is good for their way ? ð¤£

Ever read "Siddhartha" by Herman Hesse ? Try it.

What u hv said is absolutely true but still not easy to reconcile to see people around messing up their lives just for want of better understanding/perspectives. This is one area I hv not been able to address so far.

Sometimes, to NOT impart knowledge that one has, is more important. I too used to be a bit impatient to do this, do that.

[ Once I even created a Psychometrics web application, where people could "heal themselves". No one was in the least interested to even look at themselves against a psychological mirror, let alone heal. ]

I have a relative who has an inherited (mild) problem. It leads to lack of sleep, irritability. He knows, and his family requests him, to take a mild sleeping pill like Alzolam, or a mild anti-depressive like Serlift. But no. He is angry, he does not want to "appear unbalanced". The actual effect is, every year I see him becoming more and more unbalanced.

From this I realized - people choose their own paths, own fates. Even your nearest and dearest. There is NOTHING we can do for others. All we can do is for ourselves.

One of the issues or unrest I face is that I want to Impart to people around me the understanding/insights/knowledge/realisations to help them overcome the struggle that they hv made of this thing called life. Any understanding or realisations I immediately feel like taking it to the world. I feel with a little bit of knowledge and understanding people can potentially reduce their suffering which they undergo due to lack of understanding for example a small scratch on their car brings humungous suffering to some people newer or bigger the car higher the suffering. So difficult to make people understand the impermanence of everything they are worried about or running after.

As Shakespeare said

All the world's a stage, and all the men and women merely players; / They have their exits and their entrances; / And one man in his time plays many parts

It is a perfectly correct understanding, and my own is not too different.

There is some kind of "work" being done here, a yagna. We are all ordinary workers who do not really understand the whole, and do not even need to. We should just hope that we understand well our own part. Because only that way, promotion will come ð

Attributing all that we do to God - tera tujhko arpan kya laage mera (from our aarti om jai jagdish hare).

We are a branch office of  God and sent by him to do hustle to make this world better one day the branch office will close and go back and merge with the HO (God) and depending on how well or badly we did our next assignment will be decided (punarjanma and law of karma) or we may be absorbed in the HO itself (moksha).

This is my rudimentary understanding of things
Somehow, oneself and "others" both have to be carried/balanced on the journey, and yet the journey is for the sake of God. Frankly I too cannot see how this works out in practice.

Very much in the 2nd category cherishing the vagaries of life.

There is a famous poem by ramdhari Singh dinkarji where he says to yudhisthir

Dharamraj sanyas khojna kayarta hai mann ki
Hai sachha manujatva suljhana granthiyan is jeevan ki

In the same poem he later adds

Kriya dharam ko chhor manuj kaise nij sukh payega
Karm rahega Saath Bhaag to jahan kahin bhi jayega

And the following lines hv formed the philosophy the basis of my life

Ek panth hai chhor jagat ko apne Mein ram jao
Khojo nij ki mukti nij ko hi sukhi banao

Apar panth hai (the alternate way) auron ko bhi nij vivek bal dekar
Pahuncho upar swarg Mein apne Saath bahut ko Lekar.

It depends. A shallow or partial understanding may leave one with a sense of "sanyas le leta huun, kya rakha hae ye sab mein". But one step forward, this life itself, with its simplest pleasures, like eating bread with jam, or just watching a squirrel, may become a fabulous experience.

It is for this joy in simple things that people pursue meaning and understanding. Not for any "higher things".

The only piece of actual life that exists is the one u r passing thru right now nothing more
